points.text: Biodexa Pharmaceuticals is advancing MTX240, a molecular glue therapeutic candidate designed for the treatment of gastrointestinal stromal tumors.
MTX240 functions by bringing two intracellular proteins, PDE3A and SLFN12, into close proximity to form a stable complex within GIST cancer cells.
This interaction stabilizes SLFN12, enabling it to drive RNase-mediated apoptosis in GIST cells through a mechanism independent of KIT signaling.
A-Alpha Bio leverages its AlphaSeq and AlphaBind platforms to engineer next-generation dual-specific and molecular glue therapeutics.
Tamarind Bio has partnered with A-Alpha Bio to provide seamless access to the AlphaSeq platform for experimental validation of computationally designed binders.
Biodexa in-licensed exclusive worldwide rights to develop and commercialize MTX240 from Otsuka Pharmaceutical in February 2026.
The company appointed Syngene to manufacture GMP clinical trial supplies for the upcoming Phase 1b/2a study of MTX240.
Molecular glue technology represents a novel approach that induces targeted protein interactions distinct from conventional tyrosine kinase inhibitors.
Biodexa announced preliminary results for the year ended 31 December 2025, highlighting the strategic transition to higher value therapeutics.
The Phase 1b/2a study is expected to enroll patients with TKI-resistant GIST to validate MTX240's potential in high-need populations.
Symeres and Ambagon Therapeutics have entered into a collaboration agreement.
The partnership focuses specifically on molecular glue research initiatives.
The targeted therapeutic area for this research is colorectal cancer.
This development was publicly reported on April 02, 2026.
The announcement was covered by Pharmabiz news sources.
